summary: Full regression of API Keys feature (backend + frontend). 13/13 backend pytest pass + 1 skipped (RBAC – no non-admin seeded user). All 7 frontend UI flows pass: page load, create+reveal-once, copy/done buttons, masked list display, active-toggle (Active <-> Inactive), edit (rename + add allowed endpoint), revoke with confirm dialog. JWT regression on POST/PUT /api/accounts/{id}/invoices preserved.

minor.issue: Could not verify non-admin -> 403 because no non-admin seeded user is documented in /app/memory/test_credentials.md (only admin). Code review confirms _can_manage() only allows {System Admin, CEO, Director, admin, Admin} and is enforced on every route.

action_items: Optional: seed a non-admin user (e.g. AccountManager role) in test_credentials.md so RBAC negative-path can be auto-verified.
Optional: include 'list_master_skus' or 'list_accounts' in the pytest external auth happy-path (currently only POST/PUT invoice exercised end-to-end).
critical_code_review_comments: routes/api_keys.py authenticate_api_key correctly hashes incoming key (sha256) before lookup; raw key never logged; key_hash & raw key both excluded from list/get serialization.
deps.get_user_or_api_key uses a lazy import to break the routes/api_keys.py <-> deps.py circular import — clean.
Endpoint matching uses regex built from path_pattern with {var} -> [^/]+; safe and method-checked. Trailing slash normalized.
Tenant context is pinned via core.tenant.set_current_tenant(key_doc.tenant_id) — good multi-tenant isolation.
ApiKeysPage.js: Bearer header reads localStorage 'token' OR 'session_token' — matches existing app convention. Reveal modal correctly displays full key once and shows masked dots in list.
Minor: list-route uses GET '' (empty path under /api-keys prefix). Works but a trailing-slash redirect could surprise some HTTP clients; non-blocking.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed keys and clean them up via session-scoped fixture; verified 0 TEST_ keys remain after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression file is /app/backend/tests/test_api_keys_external.py — re-run with `pytest backend/tests/test_api_keys_external.py -v`. The test for non-admin RBAC uses pytest.skip() if no non-admin login works; if a non-admin seed user is added to /app/memory/test_credentials.md, update the candidate list inside TestRoleProtection.
